event_info: belted straightaway! Expectedly Deepak Chahar goes for the away-swinger to the LHB but he dropped it way too short so Jaiswal rocked back to smash the pull through mid-wicket

now he smacks it over the top. Deepak Chahar bowls fuller while trying to swing it away. Jaiswal clears the front leg out of the way and muscles it over mid-wicket for the first maximum of the day. DC straightaway asks the captain for a man at deep mid-wicket
sliced away over point and this is a sensational start! Full and moving away; Jaiswal stayed leg-side of this ball to access the off-side. Man in the ring jumped but it was too high for a catch
flicked and he finds the man at mid-wicket. Full and on the leg stump; Jaiswal mistimes the shot and finds the fielder
it's a low full toss and these gifts will be accepted gleefully. Jaiswal says thank you very much and belts it over the top and hits it straight down the ground. Few bounces and into the fence
that is a poor ball right in the slot; another gift and Jaiswal lines up for a big cover drive and picks the gap perfectly for four more. 22 runs off the first over

doesn't matter if you are Bumrah. If it's in the slot; I am whacking it into the stands. Bumrah misses the mark and offers slot; Vaibhav with his lovely backlift; flicks it with style over wide long-on and Jasprit Bumrah just smiles and walks back to his mark
slower delivery on the leg stump; Vaibhav flicks to mid-wicket
round the to Jaiswal; flicked through deep square leg

the clarity he has at the crease is amazing to watch. Shortish slower delivery down the leg-side. Vaibhav rocks back and swivels while pulling this high and deep into the stands beyond backward square leg

oh he has missed out on a full toss. Vaibhav misses the flick; ball hits the pad and rolls to short fine leg. Looks for a run that isn't there. Throw is off target at the striker's end and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was lazy to get back and he eventually falls on the stumps while gaining ground
full toss; Vaibhav flicks and finds mid-wicket
Trent Boult tried the yorker but offered slot and on the pads; Jaiswal flicks and whips it over deep backward square leg

Trent Boult drags the length back and Jaiswal rocks back to pull over square leg this time. Everything is almost getting into the upper tier. Slightly around middle and leg and Jaiswal had his intentions clear to smash the ball over the ropes
gets the yorker right on the leg stump; flicked to mid-wicket

another yorker missed and yorkers are the only way to stop these two is the feeling for MI and they have missed their mark quite often to offer to slot. That back lift of his makes it easy to whip on the leg-side and he sends it soaring over backward square leg
too wide of off stump says the ump
length delivery on the off stump; Vaibhav mistimes the loft along the ground to long-on
great save diving to the left by Hardik. Jaiswal delayed the bat swing to smash this slower ball down the ground. Hit it towards mid-off so Hardik was able to cut it off
oh he wanted to scoop; but Hardik fired this short ball outside off stump. Jaiswal tried to change his shot and send it over the off-side but missed
very full delivery on the leg stump; Jaiswal flicks to deep mid-wicket
short of length delivery wide of off stump; cut to deep point
shortish delivery; Jaiswal pulls to long-on
length delivery on the leg stump; Vaibhav flicks to deep square leg

oh great slower ball! Second time Jaiswal tries to scoop to the leg-side and Hardik Pandya forces him to change the shot to the off-side by bowling slightly outside off stump. This time he took pace off and it brushed the pad on the way to Ryan Rickelton
back to offering slot balls in the name of trying the yorker. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi anyway has enough fun and he is absolutely enjoying these gifts. Hits it clear and sweet over cover
Shardul takes pace off but he still offers slot. Vaibhav delays the bat swing to connect and this time he hits it over cover for one bounce four

the good old hard length and Vaibhav gets an under edge that just escapes the stumps to roll to short fine leg. It's called hard length for a reason and it's funny how so easily teams overlook this length for the glory yorker
slower delivery outside off stump; Jaiswal delays the drive but slices it to deep point

just one ball in the hard length region. It offered only one run and for some reason Shardul Thakur shies away from it. Dishes out an absolute smack me ball. Vaibhav says yeah; I'd love it there all day and muscles it towards the sightscreen

ball_event: out Caught by Tilak Varma!! And they have bought this wicket with some smart field placing. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi covers his face in disappointment that he's picked deep extra cover. This is a full wide slot ball
event_info: Vaibhav absolutely nailed this hard. The problem was he hit it too flat. Full marks to Tilak to having to leap to the left and take this overhead despite the speed on the ball. The momentum takes him slightly backwards but the rope was still a distance away. Great innings and a good catch from Tilak. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi c Tilak Varma b Shardul Thakur 39(14) [4s-1 6s-5]
fires this wide of leg stump
wristed through mid-wicket
flatter delivery on the leg stump; Jurel flicks through mid-wicket and they race back for two
oh beaten! Spins away from the RHB; who was looking to cut and it beats the outside edge

ball_event: out Lbw!! Dhruv Jurel stood back and missed a straight ball. Seemed like he was beaten for pace. Wanted to flick through mid-wicket but the ball sneak past the inside edge. Impact was in line with middle and leg and Ghazanfar let out a confident shout
event_info: up went the finger. Jurel knew his fate but he chanced the review and takes one with him. Dhruv Jurel lbw b AM Ghazanfar 2(3)
flatter delivery; Parag pushes it along the ground to long-off
spoils the over himself by bowling a full ball outside off stump; Jaiswal gets under it and smacks it over cover to make it 9 off the over
watchfully flicks this short of length delivery through mid-on
oh tried to be cheeky with a late cut; almost beat short third man for four but Shardul managed to dive to right and cut it off
short slower delivery; Jaiswal pulls to deep mid-wicket

this is so cunning from Riyan. Played that so; so late with angled bat. Oh wow; he's actually defended that ball late and wasn't the usual guide. The kind of defence that Kane Williamson plays so late to pinch a single to third man. Bumrah smiles and walks back as he knows it was a smart shot
short slower delivery; Parag pulls to mid-wicket
yorker; Jaiswal digs it out towards mid-off
full toss; Jaiswal gently drives to long-off
Parag wanted to whip it over long leg but missed out. Inside edge onto pad and rolling to short fine leg

short and wide outside off stump; cuts it hard and it rockets to the deep point boundary. Back-to-back fifties for Jaiswal. Raises the bat to the crowd and the dugout. Launched into Deepak Chahar in the first over and set the tone for RR
good ball! Followed Jaiswal as he backed away; whipped to long-on on the bounce
oh tried to be too cute by leaving a wide yorker but it wasn't a wide. Precious dot for MI

in the air; Shardulll; Shardullll and it's just over his acrobatic leap! Full toss as Hardik Pandya misses the yorker; Parag whipped that with style so it went high than long. Shardul moved to the right at deep mid-wicket. He was in play for a good part but he couldn't get his hands on it despite a dive. In fact replays show he got hands to it but couldn't hold on or parry the ball back into the playing area
full ball; Jaiswal leaves nothing in the tank and goes real hard down the ground. Ball goes off the bottom of the bat and lands just inside the rope
on the pads; Jaiswal swings too hard; gets an inside edge that rolls to short fine leg
oh under edge onto Ryan Rickelton's pads. No way the keeper can catch those. Flatter delivery; Parag looks to slash and gets away with the under edge
this is high and long enough over long-on. Slot ball; Parag gets under it and gets decent power behind the shot to get another maximum

ball_event: out Caught by Tilak Varma!! This is flat
event_info: long-on in business and taken. Parag thought he could have another six down the ground. Cleared the front leg out of the way and attempted the six; but the ball went off the bottom of the bat and stayed well inside the rope. Riyan Parag c Tilak Varma b AM Ghazanfar 20(10) [4s-1 6s-2]
flatter delivery; Hetmyer cuts to point and keeps strike
starts with a dot! Slower delivery angling across; Hetmyer lets it go as he couldn't line it up
yorker on the stumps; Hetmyer digs it out square of the wicket for a run
tries to beat him with a slower bumper but the square leg ump didn't like the height on that

that is muscled! Even Bumrah can miss yorkers folks. It's such a tough ball and the moment he saw the ball make contact with the bat he threw his back in disappointment that he offered a slot ball. Jaiswal sends it soaring over the sightscreen and someone in the stands dropped the catch
mistimed to long-off
short slower delivery outside off stump; Hetmyer looks to pull and misses
Hetmyer had no clue that it would arrive today and he finished his slog yesterday. Great slower ball; toe-ended to the right of the bowler for a single
starts off with a full wide ball; Hetmyer mistimes the slog to the on-side and they get two as the man from the deep had to run forward to mid-wicket

tries the wide yorker and it's outside off stump. Oh it's not been given and Hety wants it checked. That's well outside the line and Kumar Dharmasena has to reverse the call. Looked wide to the naked eye
great yorker; Hetmyer mistimes it to mid-wicket off the inside edge

that's a full toss wide of off stump. And tell you what; full credit to Yashasvi Jaiswal for trying to open the face and guide the ball in the final ball of the innings when it was easy for him to swing too hard given the situation of the game. Third man up so he just guides it to the left of that fielder and gets four

that's too high. Slower ball bumper goes over the head. MI chance their review; just a waste of time as Jaiswal is leaping and it is still well over his head. They check for the catch as well but the ball is away from the bat
brilliant off-side yorker; Jaiswal tries to squeeze it out but misses. Appeal for catch but the ump says no. No bat on it

smart! Finally Jaiswal connects with the scoop. He knew Shardul Thakur was only going to try the wide yorker; so he moves off-side to unsettle the bowler and the bowler bangs it into the wicket to make it easy with the scoop. Fine to the right of Ryan Rickelton for four more
back to the wide yorker and too wide
full and outside off stump; in the slot and Jaiswal gets under it to smack it over cover. He does the fist pump as he absolutely enjoyed thrashing the ball tonight.
139.5ks short of length delivery; Rickelton rides the bounce and pats it towards backward point
fuller in length; it was there for Rohit to loft through the line; he does exactly that but ekes out an inside-edge onto the pad; the ball rolls to cover and the pair cross over
back of a length delivery; no width on offer and Rickelton can only knock it towards cover
138.7ks full and swinging in; Rohit picks it off the pads but there's protection at deep mid-wicket; just a single. Archer wanted Jadeja to come for the catch but the ball landed well in front
there goes Rickelton. First for Mumbai Indians. 138.8ks length delivery; around middle and leg; Rickelton picks it nonchalantly and desposts it into the deep mid-wicket stand

ball_event: out Caught by Dhruv Jurel!! Archer strikes back! Excellent catch from Dhruv Jurel. That ball went high and took some time to come down
event_info: but Jurel takes his cap off to get a better sight of the ball; runs back a few yards and hangs on with both hands. 140.2ks back of a length; angled across the left-hander and Rickelton attempted his favourite heave to the leg-side; gets more of the top-edge and then Jurel comes into the picture. Ryan Rickelton c Dhruv Jurel b Jofra Archer 8(4) [6s-1]

124.1ks back of a length delivery; Rohit rides the bounce and punches it in front of square; beats a diving cover-point but the ball does not have enough to race away. Jaiswal chases it down and hauls it in with a full stretch dive; saves a run

well; well! First ball and Suryakumar plays the lap. No sighters in such a short game. 143.2ks leg-lined; Suryakumar sits down inside the line and uses the pace on offer to lap it all the way over fine leg
sprays it down leg; Suryakumar misses the glance and Kumar Dharmasena signals a wide
143.8ks good length ball around off; Suryakumar camps back and angles it to backward point

ball_event: out Caught by Jofra Archer!! Gone! This is a massive wicket. Suryakumar departs cheaply. Burger roars looking at SKY. 125.5ks pace off short of length delivery
event_info: Suryakumar swivels to play the pull but since there's no pace; he does not get the power to clear deep backward square. Archer bends down; sits on his knees and reverse cups it safely. This is turning into an excellent start for the Royals. Suryakumar Yadav c Jofra Archer b Nandre Burger 6(3) [6s-1]
144.1ks fullish delivery and Tilak is drawn into the drive; squirts an inside-edge backward of square leg and opens his account with a brace
charges down and is met by a 126.7ks slower ball; no width as well and Tilak stabs it to the right of Burger
fullish delivery around off; Rohit pushes it off the outer half to backward point
speared into the blockhole; Rohit brings his bat down and jams it to short third. Tilak not keen on the single

ball_event: out Lbw!! That's crashing into the base of middle-stump. Not sure why Rohit reviewed that. Mumbai Indians are three down and lose a review as well. This is brilliant from Sandeep. He has brought Jurel up to the stumps
event_info: then attacks the sticks. He's bowled a perfect yorker and Rohit premeditated the lap; gets into a tangle and falls over while playing the shot; misses it completely and is struck on the back leg. He had shuffled across and was hit in-line with the stumps. Simple decision for Mohit Krishnadas but Rohit still decided to send it upstairs. Rohit Sharma lbw b Sandeep Sharma 5(6)
good length ball close to off; Hardik drives on the up towards mid-off and Parag makes a fine stop; saves a few
angled in from round the wicket; no room on offer and Tilak eases it to the right of Sandeep
121ks darted down leg; Tilak tries to help it along and does not connect. Wide called. Excellent take by Jurel
108.5ks slower back of a length delivery; Tilak punches to long-on. Just 3 runs and the wicket of Rohit
Mumbai Indians need a move on. Deshpande starts with a slower ball - 112.9ks outside off; Tilak fetches it from there and whips it over mid-on; one bounce and over the ropes
121.1ks short and banged into the surface; Tilak miscues the pull and gets enough for the ball to clear the infield. Poor running as a second was definitely on but Hardik wasn't interested
in the air and in the gap. Full pitcher around off; there for the drive and Hardik did not bother to keep it down; creams it through extra cover and watches the ball race away

122.1ks slower short of length delivery; Hardik gives everything on the slash and still does not connect. The ball bounces on the way to Jurel. Hardik looks at the square leg umpire for height and is not happy it wasn't given one short ball for the over
1 run; slower bouncer; Hardik lets it go and Jurel fumbles as the ball bounces in front of him. Still no wide as Hardik had bent his knees
off-pace delivery; Tilak chops it to sweeper cover and retains strike
very full delivery on leg; Tilak digs it out to mid-wicket and Bishnoi chases it himself

shot! That's more like it from Hardik. Short and outside off; he stays leg-side of the ball and gives himself the space to have a clean flow of the hands; crashes it in front of square and gives sweeper no chance

ball_event: out Caught by Yashasvi Jaiswal!! Bishnoi strikes in his first over once again! Did that against GT as well when he removed a well-set Sudarshan
event_info: today gets the dangerous Hardik. 101.4ks quicker delivery; but the length is held back; Hardik thinks it is in the slot and lofts through the line. He gets it high on the bat and Jaiswal runs to his left from long-on to take a good catch. He was on the move when he completed the catch. Hardik Pandya c Yashasvi Jaiswal b Ravi Bishnoi 9(6) [4s-2]
97.4ks googly around off; Dhir leans forward and works it through mid-wicket
excellent placement! 93ks wrong 'un that's dragged short; Tilak hangs back and powers the pull in front of square; splitting deep mid-wicket and long-on perfectly

ball_event: out Caught by Shimron Hetmyer!! Second wicket in the over and Mumbai Indians are tottering. Half the side is back in the hut. Bishnoi is pumped. Another googly
event_info: which is full and at the stumps; Tilak sits down to play the slog sweep and does not quite get under the length; miscues it to deep mid-wicket and Hetmyer takes a simple catch. Tilak Varma c Shimron Hetmyer b Ravi Bishnoi 14(10) [4s-2]
uhhh! That didn't bounce. Sandeep's slingy action does not allow the ball to bounce; Dhir is camping back and the ball sneaks under his bat. Luckily for him it was not on target
121.5ks good length ball at the stumps; Dhir drives wide of long-on and Jaiswal charges in; fumbles and concedes the extra run
smashed! Slower ball banged into the pitch; sits up nicely outside off and Dhir nails the pull; he gets it off the meat and the ball sails over the cow corner fence

run-out chance missed. Rutherford had given up. Dhir toe-ends the drive and the ball rolls straight to backward point. Jadeja has plenty of time and he takes aim at the non-striker's end; misses the target and Rutherford survives. He had given up and fortunately Jadeja did not hit the stumps
edged and four. Fullish delivery; not much pace on offer and Dhir throws the kitchen sink on the big shot; the thick outside-edge flies away between Jurel and short third
full pitcher on off; Dhir presents the full face of the bat and drives it into the ground; the ball bounces past Sandeep and the fielder gets around from long-off to tidy up

dropped! Jaiswal does the hard work of getting there. But does not hang on. It was the googly; spins away a bit and Rutherford looks to go down the ground; miscues it off the toe-end and the ball skies up in front of long-off. Jaiswal charges in and gets there; goes with both hands and shells it
wrong 'un around leg; Rutherford nudges it into the mid-wicket pocket and races back for the second
short and spinning away outside off; Rutherford lets it pass and collects the wide
in the air and wide of Bishnoi. 94.3ks flatter delivery; Rutherford works it uppishly to the left of Bishnoi
short and outside off; Dhir cracks it in front of square and picks out sweeper cover

nicely done! 94.3ks googly around off; Rutherford waits for the ball to come; opens the bat face and slices it towards backward point. Sooryavanshi dives over the ball and allows it to escape past him

just over Parag. Did he get a fingertip? Bishnoi wasn't happy with the effort. Flighted and drifted across; the length was full enough to play the drive and Rutherford hits it aerially towards extra cover. Evades a leaping Parag on the edge of the circle and collects the boundary

that's poor execution. Third man is inside the ring and Deshpande offers a short of length delivery outside off; it was pace off but Dhir just had to clear 30 yards; which he does with ease and the ball races away
135.2ks very full delivery around off; Dhir drives it down to long-on

smoked! Deshpande misses the yorker and Rutherford launches him into the stands. Full delivery; right in the slot outside off; Rutherford maintains a solid base and lofts it over deep extra cover. 92-meter six..
slower bouncer but too short and well over Rutherford; called wide for height

is there still life left in this contest? Deshpande is dishing out freebies here - full toss just below the waist; Rutherford swivels around and helps it over long leg. The line was right at the body and Rutherford did extremely well to get it over the rope

ball_event: out Caught by Sandeep Sharma!! Deshpande and the Royals are celebrating. Rutherford stands his ground. But reluctantly starts his walk back to the dugout. Excellent catch by Sandeep. Slower ball angled across
event_info: there was width on offer and Rutherford went hard on the slash; slices it to short third and the ball was dying on Sandeep; he bends down and scoops it inches above the ground. Third umpire has a look and flashes OUT on the giantscreen. Sherfane Rutherford c Sandeep Sharma b Tushar Deshpande 25(8) [4s-2 6s-2]
dipping low full toss; Thakur bends down and skews it off the toe-end; the ball lands in front of backward point and short third. Burger puts in the dive and makes the stop
neatly done! Burger comes from round the wicket and angles it in; the length is a bit short but Thakur moves across his stumps; gets under the length and laps it past short fine
fullish delivery around off; Thakur drives sans timing to long-on
on a length and outside off; Dhir drives and picks out extra cover

smart batting. Dhir is not trying to overhit his shots. He allows this ball to arrive outside off; opens the bat face and slices it over short third. He clears the inner ring comfortably and there's no stopping that

ball_event: out Caught by Ravi Bishnoi!! That should be the game. Dhir was the last recognised batter and he too departs with 13 balls left. Burger goes pace on and Dhir could not middle his big shot. 143.6ks full delivery at the stumps
event_info: Dhir clears his front leg and lofts through the line; straight down Bishnoi's throat at long-on. Naman Dhir c Ravi Bishnoi b Nandre Burger 25(13) [4s-3 6s-1]
FOUR; boundary to end the over. Strays down leg; Chahar gets inside the line and misses the glance; the ball takes a big deflection off the thigh pad and races wide of Jurel
123.8ks slower ball outside off; Thakur swings and misses. He was nowhere close to connecting..
attempted yorker gone wrong - sprays the full toss down leg and Thakur gets no bat on that; wide called
slower leg-cutter outside off; Thakur throws the kitchen sink and does not make contact. It did seem to be inside the tramline but Kumar Dharmasena signalled a wide; only for it to be overturned

finally Thakur connects. Fullish delivery and he lofts through the line; gets more height than distance. Jaiswal gets around from long-off and parries it in; Parag backs him up and sends the ball back. Together they keep it down to a couple
sprays it way outside off; no chance for Thakur to reach that; easier call for Kumar Dharmasena

ball_event: out Caught by Dhruv Jurel!! Another one bites dust. Thakur is the latest to walk back and Jadeja walks along with him
event_info: hands wrapped around him; all in a friendly manner. Length delivery; right in the slot but Thakur went so hard that he lost his shape; gets a thick top-edge and the ball goes straight up; Jurel comes in and takes it next to the stumps. Shardul Thakur c Dhruv Jurel b Sandeep Sharma 8(7) [4s-1]
on a length and outside off; Boult swipes it to long-on
short of length outside off; Chahar stands tall and slices it over the point region. Bishnoi runs across from sweeper cover; dives but fails to stop the boundary

ball_event: out Trent Boult Run Out!! Brilliant stuff from Archer. He first nails the yorker
event_info: Boult makes room and jams it down the pitch. Chahar calls for a non-existent single. Archer runs in; collects the ball and takes his time before sending in a direct-hit at the non-striker's end. Boult is nowhere close to making his ground. Trent Boult run out (Jofra Archer) 1(2)
slower ball at the stumps; Chahar stays leg-side and is early into the shot; misses and the ball lobs over the stumps
full and at the stumps; Chahar drives it to long-on
nicely done! Short of length delivery outside off; Bumrah stands tall and telescopes it over backward point. He opened the bat face and managed to clear the inner ring with ease
112.9ks slower short of length delivery; Bumrah pulls towards cow corner and settles with a single

Rajasthan Royals win by 27 runs. This is a convincing win and it makes it three in three to start the season. Short of length delivery; Chahar slices it to sweeper cover and not sure if Hetmyer spotted the ball; he hangs back instead of coming for the catch and collects it on the bounce. Happy faces in the Royals team with Parag doing a calm fist pump
